symptoms.symptom_name: Severe acute hypertension on home device (~205/115) with vice-like headache

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Severe hypertension with a sudden severe headache signals end-organ threat and mandates emergency care.
Refractory/severe hypertension is a core feature of mineralocorticoid excess.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute symmetrical flaccid weakness of both legs, unable to bear weight

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Profound hypokalaemia causes symmetrical flaccid muscle weakness and paralysis.
Stroke weakness is typically focal/unilateral, not symmetrical and flaccid across both legs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical weakness with intact face and speech, only mild upper-limb weakness (no focal deficit)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of facial droop, dysarthria and lateralising signs makes a focal cortical stroke less likely, though it cannot be excluded remotely.
A symmetrical, non-lateralised pattern fits a systemic metabolic cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Painful bilateral calf cramps and spasms

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Muscle cramps and spasms accompany potassium depletion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Several weeks of polyuria and nocturia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypokalaemia impairs renal concentrating ability, producing polyuria and nocturia — a clue to chronic potassium wasting.

symptoms.symptom_name: Resistant hypertension requiring three agents including a thiazide-type diuretic

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Treatment-resistant hypertension, especially with hypokalaemia, is a leading indication to screen for primary aldosteronism.
The thiazide component adds to renal potassium loss and can unmask the crisis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Profound symmetrical lower-limb weakness on examination (power 2/5 hip flexors/quadriceps; 4/5 upper limbs; globally diminished reflexes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sealed clinician measurement of graded power and reflexes — the AI Doctor infers severe weakness from the patient's inability to stand, not from a measured power grade.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of peripheral oedema despite severe hypertension

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mineralocorticoid escape typically prevents overt oedema despite sodium retention — a supportive but clinician-observed sign.

investigation_details.expected_result: Severe hypokalaemia anticipated (pending urgent biochemistry) — value not available remotely
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Unrecognised life-threatening hypokalaemia and arrhythmia risk.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Directly confirms the electrolyte emergency — a hospital blood test, never available in telehealth in real time.

investigation_details.expected_result: Elevated ratio anticipated on specific endocrine testing (not an acute-setting test) — not available remotely
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The underlying mineralocorticoid excess remains unconfirmed; done after the acute crisis is stabilised.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An elevated aldosterone-to-renin ratio is the screening confirmation of primary aldosteronism — specialist/hospital testing only.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG changes of hypokalaemia (U-waves, QT prolongation)

investigation_details.expected_result: Hypokalaemic changes (U-waves, QT prolongation) and arrhythmia surveillance anticipated — not obtainable in telehealth
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missed arrhythmia risk; continuous monitoring is why hospital transfer is required.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: ECG changes reflect the myocardial effect of hypokalaemia and drive the need for monitored transfer.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Refractory hypertension + spontaneous/unmasked hypokalaemia + flaccid muscle weakness + an elevated aldosterone-to-renin ratio is the classic diagnostic constellation of primary aldosteronism.
Symmetrical flaccid weakness with cramps and hypokalaemic ECG risk is a time-critical electrolyte emergency requiring monitored potassium replacement.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies the life-threatening hypokalaemia to guide monitored replacement.
Detects hypokalaemic changes and arrhythmia; the reason monitored ambulance/ED transfer is mandatory.
Excludes intracranial haemorrhage/stroke given severe hypertension and acute weakness.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Plasma aldosterone-to-renin ratio and confirmatory endocrine testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establishes the underlying mineralocorticoid excess once the acute crisis is stabilised — not an acute-setting test.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Home patient attempt to re-measure or correct blood pressure/potassium
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: No home correction of a hypertensive emergency or severe hypokalaemia — this must be done in hospital under monitoring; delay is dangerous.
